exemplar.title: Investigation Into How Far The Tower Of Pisa Can Lean Until It Fall Over

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigation Into How Far The Tower Of Pisa Can Lean Until It Fall Over

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear table of contents and numbered sections enhance navigation
Labelled figures and tables support logical development
Informative historical context effectively positions the investigation
weaknesses: Repetitive unit-conversion steps and table entries reduce conciseness
Occasional digressions and repeated sentences interrupt flow
Minor typos and an abrupt section ending detract from coherence
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent and logically developed with excellent organization (band 4 for structure, band 3 for coherence) but retains unnecessary bulk and repetition (band 2 for conciseness). Averaging these strand assessments yields a solid score of 3.

strengths: Consistent use of appropriate symbols (Î£, Â¯y, tanâ»Â¹) and units in most places
Effective integration of diagrams, tables and formulae as multiple representations
Good logical structure with numbered steps and accompanying explanations
weaknesses: Inconsistent notation for volume (n vs v) and unit conversions without explicit statements
Omitted units in some results and mis-labelled intermediate expressions
Formatting inconsistencies in superscripts, footnotes and figure references
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall the mathematical language and notation are mostly correct, multiple representations are used effectively, and the logical structure is clear (band 3 across all strands). Minor inconsistencies prevent a higher score.

strengths: Modelling the tower as stacked hollow cylinders shows significant independent thinking
Using a photograph and scaling demonstrates resourcefulness and personal initiative
Clear evidence of a personal modelling approach beyond standard templates
weaknesses: Lacks highest level of innovation or originality in approach
Missing student/supervisor details on the title page reduce contextual engagement
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work exhibits clear evidence of independent thinking, a personal approach and creativity (band 2 in all strands) but does not reach outstanding originality required for band 3.

strengths: Identification of basic modelling limitations and sources of error
Brief comparison with an external reference (National Geographic)
weaknesses: Reflection limited to superficial lists without deep discussion
Minimal quantitative analysis of errors and lack of critical insight
Evaluation of outcomes is basic with no exploration of future implications
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each reflection strand is at a basic level with only limited and superficial treatment (band 1 across all strands), warranting a score of 1.

strengths: Mathematics used is relevant and appropriately supports the aim (volumes, density, COM, trigonometry)
Correct statement and explanation of the centre-of-mass equation
Comprehensive composite modelling of hollow cylinders
weaknesses: Mathematical scope remains within pre-calculus and does not fully meet SL expectations
Repeated unit-handling errors and unjustified rounding choices
Final lean angle result shows some accuracy gaps
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Relevance of mathematics is strong (band 4), but level appropriateness and accuracy each sit at band 2. A mid-point score of 3 reflects correct and relevant use without full SL-level depth or flawless accuracy.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations usually accompany calculations, and steps are numbered, giving the mathematics a clear thread.  Occasional clutter and some mis-labelled intermediate expressions detract from complete clarity, but the logical structure remains good overall.

Understanding is sound in many places, but repeated unit-handling errors, unjustified rounding choices and an implausible final angle reveal gaps.  Accuracy is mixed, so only some understanding with errors is demonstrated.

The student employs tables, labelled diagrams, scaled sketches and formulae, integrating these representations to support the argument (e.g. diagrams of cylinders and trigonometric triangles).  The variety is useful and generally well chosen, though not exceptionally sophisticated.

The investigation follows a clear sequenceâintroduction, methodology, calculations, evaluation and conclusionâso the reader can follow the mathematical argument without getting lost.  Minor lapses in flow (occasional digressions and repeated explanatory sentences) prevent it from reaching the highest standard, but overall the logical development is solid and sustained.
Using a photograph for measurements and adapting the model for hollowness demonstrate creativity and initiative at a good, though not exceptional, level.
The student notes sources of error (photo distortion, missing columns) but the analysis of their quantitative effect is minimal.  Critical insight is basic rather than thoughtful or deep.
There is a short comparison with a National Geographic figure, yet no serious numerical reconciliation or exploration of implications.  Evaluation of outcomes therefore remains limited.
Reflection is limited to a brief strengths/limitations list; discussion of modelling assumptions is superficial and does not permeate the whole essay.  Depth remains modest.

Appropriate symbols (Î£, Â¯y, tanâ»Â¹) and units are used in most places and explanations accompany formulas.  Minor slipsâsuch as inconsistent unit notation (mÂ³ then cmÂ³ labelled ânâ)âkeep the work from total accuracy, but overall language and notation are mostly correct.

The modelling procedure is clearly the studentâs own: scaling from an image, deciding which elements to ignore, and structuring multi-stage centre-of-mass calculations.  This evidences a distinct personal approach, though not highly innovative.

The exploration is generally relevant but lengthy unit conversions and verbatim repetition of steps add unnecessary bulk (e.g. density conversion shown eight separate times).  Some paragraphs restate earlier information, so conciseness is only partial.
All mathematics (volumes, densityâmass conversion, composite centre of mass, trigonometric determination of lean) is relevant to the stated aim and used appropriately throughout the investigation.
The choice to approximate the tower as stacked hollow cylinders, derive measurements from a photo and question published lean angles shows noticeable independent thinking beyond textbook templates.

The student provides a detailed table of contents, numbered sections, labelled figures/tables and a final bibliography.  Each section is clearly sign-posted, making the structure exceptionally transparent from introduction through to appendix.
The work stays largely within pre-calculus geometry and algebra; while appropriate, it only partially reaches SL level expectations (no calculus, statistics, or advanced modelling).

annotations.comment: Several appendix entries repeat calculation headings; consider summarising repetitive content to maintain relevance and conciseness.
The title is clear in focus but reads informally; consider rephrasing âuntil it falls overâ to a more formal academic tone for coherence.
No student or supervisor details appear on the title page; adding these would strengthen presentation by situating the work.
The table of contents is exhaustive but somewhat cluttered; grouping related appendix entries would improve conciseness and navigability.
The historical context in the introduction is informative and positions the investigation effectively.
Formatting of superscripts and footnotes is inconsistent; ensure uniform mathematical notation for clarity throughout.
The assumptions section includes a typo (âin tactâ should be âintactâ); minor edits like this will enhance formality.
Figure 1 is well drawn but not explicitly referenced in this paragraph; add a clear âsee Figure 1â to link text and diagram.
The methodology is described in a single paragraph; using numbered or bulleted steps will enhance logical flow.
The cylinder section ends abruptly mid-sentence; verify that all text is complete for clear structure.
The centre-of-mass equation $$\bar y=\frac{\sum m_i y_i}{\sum m_i}$$ is correctly stated and explained.
The density formula introduces variable ânâ for volume later; maintain symbol consistency (use v rather than n).
Units mÂ³ and cmÂ³ are used without an explicit conversion statement; add step-by-step unit justification for clarity.
The volume calculation $$V=\pi(7.742)^2\times60$$ is correct but the result omits its unit (mÂ³); include units explicitly.
The large unit-conversion steps (Ã1 000 000) are repeated many times; summarise or reference a general conversion to improve conciseness.
Noting that a uniform cylinderâs centre of mass lies at half its height (30âm) is expected; reflect on this trivial result in discussion.
Using a photograph and scale demonstrates resourcefulness and initiative in data gathering.
Potential measurement errors from perspective distortion are mentioned but not quantified; consider estimating their impact.
Table 1 repeats identical calculations for stories 3â6; grouping identical rows or summarising would improve readability.
The differing radius for story 1 appears in Table 2 without explanation here; clarify this discrepancy earlier to maintain coherence.
The procedure for determining story centres could be presented with explicit subscripts $$y_i$$ to strengthen mathematical clarity.
The integration of diagrams, tables and equations exemplifies effective multiple representation.
The historical context on Archimedes is interesting but peripheral; trimming this background will focus the mathematics.
Relying on Wikipedia for the centre-of-mass definition weakens academic rigor; cite a primary physics text or journal.
Rounded value 41.048 in Table 3 contradicts standard rounding (should be 41.05); check consistency of decimal places.
The detailed appendix is transparent but oversaturated; summarise repetitive sections and refer to full details as supporting material.
The large mass-sum equation is dense and difficult to follow; breaking it into intermediate steps would enhance clarity.
The hollow-tower centre of mass (28.084âm) is computed accurately; justify rounding decisions to reinforce mathematical rigor.
Modelling the tower as hollow cylinders shows strong independent thinking and deep personal approach to realism.
